items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So it was here...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This is the research outpost you mentioned before?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: One of them. I worked at several outposts back then. But this one wasn't so lucky. The scan shows it was hit by a sudden Void Storm surge. No survivors.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So that's why I never saw them again back at the Academy.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I thought I just didn't see them around.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Aemeath...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Anything swallowed by the Void Storm slowly loses its "existence" until it's forgotten entirely.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: If a Cassette makes it out, at least there's a record. But if that gets erased too, then even the fact that they died is erased.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Use the Core, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I want to see them again.

items.speaker: Past Dazed Researcher
items.text: Aemeath, you were amazing! If not for you, that Glommoth would've crushed us to bloody pulp!

items.speaker: Past Dazed Researcher
items.text: I'm still shaking, but you're already as cool as ice… Are all Synchronists from Rabelle College as composed as you?

items.speaker: Past Outgoing Roya
items.text: If you want to thank her, share your Void Storm research! To the frostlands came our little Aemeath for this curiosity. You should show her that thesis you've been writing for years!

items.speaker: Past Meticulous Researcher
items.text: Aemeath, you think Cassettes might be a breakthrough?

items.speaker: Past Meticulous Researcher
items.text: Hm, I'm not sure… Right now they're just for archiving. Actual transcription feels like a leap.

items.speaker: Past Gentle Roya
items.text: Try looking to the Exoswarm for inspiration. In Lahai-Roi, only one power against the Void Storm exists, and that all comes from the Exostrider.

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: Haha! Your thesis or your delicious stew, either works! I'm not picky!

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: As for the Void Storm… I'll try all your ideas. The problem is, we don't understand the Void Storm deeply enough.

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: Especially in the depths with high Voidmatter concentration… No probe survives the journey, so research is stuck. I can't shake the feeling that...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: When I was a child, my parents were lost to a Void Storm. Soon after, I even forgot what they looked like.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So you see? Death hasn't been all bad!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Once I became a digital ghost, Voidmatter couldn't affect me anymore. Even if the whole world forgets my friends and colleagues, I still get to remember every single one of them.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Aemeath.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Yep?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You don't always have to smile through everything.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: ...What's that supposed to mean?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I'm happy. Really. It's the way I choose to live. You're too serious, always overthinking, hence the long face.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You wouldn't understand how happy this moment makes me.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Come on. I'll show you the frostlands' slumbering giant. Lahai-Roi's… Exostrider.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hm... I knew the next stop would be here.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The cabin up ahead... It's where I used to live with my family. I—

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Sorry. I just... I need a minute. I'm not ready yet.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Just like before. The moment it appears, my frequency starts getting sucked out of me.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Aemeath said it's a Threnodian derivative, like the Voidworm. They share similar frequencies, but… my gut says it's different.)

items.speaker: Sigillum
items.text: Go home...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: What happened to you?!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Your frequency's been leaking everywhere! I tried plugging it from the inside, but it didn't work.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Eurgh! No, this is no good... How about this!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: It-it stopped... for now. I gotta... I gotta rest...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You did great, Abby... Go get some rest.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Don't you ever jump in after me like that again.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The Voidmatter concentration down there is too high. If you fall into <color=RedD>Voidspace</color>... even you could be consumed. Erased from existence.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Always making people worry.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: There's a theory in Lahai-Roi: Threnodian Aleph-1, the Devourer of Existence, is the embodiment of voidance. The dissolution of all things.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Its true form is like... a <color=RedD>black hole</color>.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And that theory is correct. I verified it myself.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So far, I'm the only one who's reached it. Because I'm already dead, and no living soul could withstand a place like that.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You went inside the Threnodian itself?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Yes. Think of a severe Void Storm as a manifestation and portal into Voidspace itself—A higher dimensional plane. It's pitch-black, but also like a kaleidoscope.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Inside, <color=RedD>time and space aren't linear. Everything is warped.</color>

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Things the Threnodian devoured from different eras can be sitting right next to each other.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I know you've fought many Threnodians, but Aleph-1 is different. It's already won, elsewhere....

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Wandering through that <te href=750040>Voidspace</te>, I saw the proof... a graveyard of civilizations it's already ended.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The Threnodian's presence in Lahai-Roi is only a sliver of its true self. It's been trying to break through the seal on the <te href=750036>Stridergate</te> to devour Solaris.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So... {PlayerName}, don't go wandering off alone. Not until the Exostrider awakens.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: All good on your end, golden boy? Lucilla mentioned the Exostrider in the frostlands just moved. Please tell me that was you?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That was Aemeath. Given how well you know me, I assume you know her, too.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: ...You found her?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I brought her back to the Academy. It's a long story. I'll fill you in when there's time.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Understood. But I do have to ask, on Lucilla's behalf: what's the status of the Core?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It was returned to the Exostrider.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: That explains it. The Research Institute is reporting a spike in Voidmatter concentrations over Lahai-Roi. Void Storm activity is kicking up again...

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Back when the Reactor Drive was in the sky, its annual self-diagnostics caused the Exostrider's Greatsword pointed at the Threnodian to weaken.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: The Exostrider is probably running the same procedure now to integrate what you returned. Best for you both to stay within the Academy until peak season passes.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Alright, I'll be careful.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Next time you're in the infirmary, we should revisit your old plan. The one tied to the Exostrider, the Stridergate, and the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: For now… just be with Aemeath. It must be a great relief to have her back. I'm happy for you.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: You've earned a break, {PlayerName}. Get some rest.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: This is incredible. Having company like this... The boredom I felt as a ghost could've literally killed me again.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Wanna go up top to feel the breeze?

options.text: I said I'd spend the day with you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Ah… I'm so happy right now. Really.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Even a fake sky can hold such beauty.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Aemeath still isn't back, but if my hunch is right...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Aemeath, have you been out terrorizing those <te href=750041>Snowfluff Seals</te> again? They scatter the moment they see you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Keep this up, and it won't be long before there isn't anyone left in the frostlands to play with...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Have you finished your lessons? You start school soon. Maybe spend less time gaming, hm?

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: Nah-uh, they love playing with me! Why else would they crawl over and play before running away?

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: And my lessons—ahem, {PlayerName}, you promised me another level of Split Stars! No take-backs!

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: But... I want to learn how you folded that thing first. Can you show me? Pretty please?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You mean... the paper plane?

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: Yeah! Mine always nose-dives right away, but yours flew so far! How?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Hehe, of course there's a trick to it.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'll teach you. But first, you finish your lessons. Then we'll fold planes together, alright?

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: ...Oh, okay. You promise?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I promise. Cross my heart.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I've been wondering how to tell you this. And now...

options.text: You can tell me now, Aemeath.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: When I was little, I woke up one morning and realized I couldn't remember the sound of my parents' laughter anymore.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Their voices, their faces, their warmth... everything about them dissolved in the Void Storm.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Later, even the memory of forgetting them began to fade from my mind.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: It terrified me... to the bone.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I didn't know how to fight it. I felt cold, and I knew I had to do something, anything, to make it stop.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So I ran away from Bjartr Woods by myself. I hid in the Starward Riseway and made my way to the frostlands.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: My parents... One was a foreign researcher, the other a Roya. They were stationed here, studying the Void Storm.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I wanted to return to where we once lived, but I couldn't remember the way. I stumbled around for so long... until I finally reached this lake.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: My past self saved you...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I was sinking into the lake. Choking. Everything was fading... until a pair of warm hands caught me.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: They held on tight and pulled me out… and when I opened my eyes, I saw you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But then I realized the talisman my parents had left me was gone. Probably lost to the deep. The thought alone broke my heart.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I cried so hard, and you had no choice but to scoop me up, comforting me as best you could. You took me back to your cabin to get warm.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And after that... Well, you guessed it. I stayed with you there, under your care, until the day you left Lahai-Roi.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Why did you keep this from me?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Because it would've been awkward.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Imagine someone you don't remember runs up to you, all smiles, saying, "Long time no see! I'm the kid you used to look after!"...Haha!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And what would I even say next? "Wanna go visit our old cabin?" "I listened to you and topped my class!" Or—"Yeah, I accidentally got myself killed, my bad!"

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Saying any of that... would only burden you. You never asked for any of it.

options.text: I don't see it that way.
You've always felt familiar to me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Since I woke in the Gorges of Spirits, I've met many people from my past. They've helped me recover pieces of myself. It's a good thing.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I can make my own choices. You should have a little more faith in me, Aemeath.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: ...I see.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Then I'll just shoot away! My freshman speech, my first song release, the time I hit a new sync rate record... You missed all the big moments!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: ...But I couldn't be mad. I knew you were busy.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You were always off saving Solaris from the Lament. You had to, and I do think that's a good thing.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So I thought, why can't I do the same? Look! I kept Lahai-Roi safe while you were away.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Sure, there was a cost. But not bad for your unofficial apprentice, right?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The more you save, the steeper the cost. I've always known that. But some things, you just gotta do.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You think so? Hearing that from a big ol' savior like you... Well, that's nice.

options.text: How have you been these years?
You really never saw me before?
If that's really your wish, then...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You mean after you left? Well… pretty normal, I'd say. Went to school, enjoyed my days. Just like you wanted me to.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And then... I became a ghost. Since then, it's been… drifting between the Academy and Voidspace.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: See, there is a bright side! Dying gave me a free pass into Voidspace. I'd call that a win. Gotta explore somewhere, right?

options.text: There's something else I want to ask.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Saw you? Oh, you mean that thing you brought up in class? About me helping you take down the Eye of the Threnodian?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Honestly, I don't remember a thing. But if the Threnodian was involved, it must've been in Voidspace.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: …Anyway, no clues from me. Threnodians having weird powers is nothing new. Or maybe you were just seeing things.

options.text: I have something else to ask you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hey, easy now. Just rest here tonight. We'll handle everything tomorrow when you're stronger.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Project "rebuilding myself" is going great. Once we reach the Exostrider and recharge with the Core, we can make for Sigillum and finish it for good.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I will take care of the problem for you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We will face it together, Aemeath.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Of course! But for now, bedtime. You take your old room. I'll be in mine.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Kinda takes me back, saying goodnight like this. Just like old times.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: ...Good night!

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: ...Huh? When did I fall asleep...

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: My paper plane... I have to show it to {PlayerName}...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: For years, the little website I threw together to post songs kept getting comments.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: They'd talk about how much they loved a song, or share problems they were having in their lives. And then... they started connecting with each other.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I never saw myself as a hitmaker. I was just an amateur, singing what was in my heart.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But over time, they stopped being just my songs. They became... a melody that connected people.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: If my listeners want a complete song, I'll finish it for them… or maybe for me, too.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You really do care about the people of Lahai-Roi, don't you, Aemeath?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I guess I do now. Wouldn't have said that at the start, though.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: To be honest, I never had some grand passion. Before I became a ghost, I just wanted to live an easy, happy life and try a little bit of everything.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And my whole "I want to be a savior" thing? Well, that was just me trying to copy you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But after I died, I had all this time. Time to notice the people and things in Lahai-Roi I'd overlooked before.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So many people here dream of becoming stars themselves, but that was never my dream.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I'd rather be the one who looks up at the stars. The one who watches over them.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Really? But the way I see it...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Go on then, Aemeath. I want to hear you sing.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You'd better! You are the one I most want to hear me.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But first… hmm, you need a better look for the show.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: There! Brand new, just like I remember you wearing. Looks good, right? A new place calls for a new look.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Ah, this brings me back...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I remember when you used to wear this around the Academy. You'd leave a whole trail of swooning admirers in your wake, Solaris's great savior!

options.text: Alright, that's enough out of you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Haha! What, are you blushing? Since when is having charm a bad thing?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Now, go grab that seat over there. Best view in the house. I scouted it myself back when I used to nap through lectures.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: As for me... it's showtime.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: For years, the little website I threw together to post songs kept getting comments.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: They'd talk about how much they loved a song, or share problems they were having in their lives. And then... they started connecting with each other.

items.speaker: Aemeath

items.text: So many people here dream of becoming stars themselves, but that was never my dream.

items.speaker: Aemeath

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Really? But the way I see it...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Go on then, Aemeath. I want to hear you sing.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You'd better! You are the one I most want to hear me.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But first… hmm, you need a better look for the show.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: There! Brand new, just like I remember you wearing. Looks good, right? A new place calls for a new look.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Ah, this brings me back...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I remember when you used to wear this around the Academy. You'd leave a whole trail of swooning admirers in your wake, Solaris's great savior!

options.text: Alright, that's enough out of you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Haha! What, are you blushing? Since when is having charm a bad thing?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Now, go grab that seat over there. Best view in the house. I scouted it myself back when I used to nap through lectures.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: As for me... it's showtime.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Look, we've reached the Exostrider's feet.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Once I find more pieces of my existence here, I'll be strong enough to face Sigillum…

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: We take that thing down, and this is all over! Alright, let's race full-speed ahead to the finale!
